steps.title: Write the quadratic
steps.content:
Expand the equation and move all terms to one side.
$$x(kx-36)=-9$$
$$kx^2-36x+9=0$$

steps.title: Require two real solutions
steps.content:
A quadratic has two distinct real solutions when its discriminant is positive. Here, $a=k$, $b=-36$, and $c=9$.
$$(-36)^2-4(k)(9)>0$$

steps.title: Find the greatest integer
steps.content:
Solve the inequality.
$$1{,}296-36k>0$$
$$1{,}296>36k$$
$$36>k$$
The greatest integer less than $36$ is $35$. The answer is 35.
